shorts.headline: Will AI End Coding As We Know It?
shorts.narration: Tech journalist Clive Thompson interviewed 70+ devs at Google, Amazon, and Microsoft. His explosive conclusion? AI is reshaping programming forever. 'Coding After Coders'—could this be the future? Stay ahead of the curve! Follow for daily AI updates.

shorts.headline: Hidden Deep-Sea Proteins Supercharge Tests
shorts.narration: Scientists discovered ultra-tough DNA-binding proteins in volcanic lakes and deep-sea vents. These molecules survive extreme heat, salt, and chemicals—revolutionizing disease detection. Nature’s secrets might just save lives! Follow for daily AI updates.

shorts.headline: Meta Delays AI Model Over Performance Issues
shorts.narration: Meta’s new AI division hit a snag—delaying its rollout due to performance concerns. Their only product so far? Vibes, an AI video app like OpenAI’s Sora. Will they bounce back? Follow for daily AI updates.

shorts.headline: Iran’s Space Research Centre Damaged in Strikes
shorts.narration: Video footage reveals severe damage to Iran’s Space Research Centre after Israeli-US strikes. The fallout could reshape regional tech ambitions. Geopolitics meets space race—stay tuned! Follow for daily AI updates.

shorts.headline: Samsung Browser Gets AI Boost in One UI 9
shorts.narration: Samsung Browser is leveling up with multitasking and AI features in One UI 9. With Chrome dominating, can Samsung carve its niche? The browser wars heat up! Follow for daily AI updates.

shorts.headline: Fed Holds Rates as Iran War Roils Markets
shorts.narration: The US Fed is expected to hold rates steady amid Iran war chaos. Oil prices and market uncertainty loom large. Will policymakers navigate the storm? Follow for daily AI updates.

shorts.headline: Open-Source Tool Supercharges NVIDIA GPUs for LLMs
shorts.narration: GreenBoost—an open-source Linux module—augments NVIDIA GPU memory with system RAM and NVMe storage. The goal? Run larger AI models effortlessly. Innovation at its finest! Follow for daily AI updates.

shorts.headline: Kernel Anti-Cheats: Gaming’s Secret Guardians
shorts.narration: Modern kernel anti-cheats are ultra-sophisticated, operating at the highest privilege level in Windows. They’re the invisible shield keeping games fair. But at what cost? Follow for daily AI updates.

shorts.headline: Court Upholds Firing After Brain Tumor Leave
shorts.narration: Delhi High Court ruled against a director terminated post-brain tumor surgery, stating no right to a 3-year leave. A harsh reality check for employee rights. Follow for daily AI updates.

long_video.headline: AI’s Takeover of Programming: The Clive Thompson Report
long_video.narration: Tech journalist Clive Thompson’s groundbreaking interviews with 70+ developers reveal a seismic shift—AI is rewriting the rules of coding. From Google to startups, the consensus is clear: programming will never be the same. Dive into the future of tech with us.

long_video.headline: Deep-Sea Proteins: The Next Medical Breakthrough?
long_video.narration: Hidden in Earth’s most extreme environments, scientists uncovered ultra-resilient DNA-binding proteins. These discoveries could revolutionize disease testing, offering stability under brutal conditions. How will this reshape medicine? Let’s explore.

long_video.headline: Meta’s AI Struggles: Vibes and Delays
long_video.narration: Meta’s AI ambitions hit a roadblock as performance concerns delay their new model. With Vibes as their sole product, can they compete with giants like OpenAI? The stakes have never been higher.

long_video.headline: Iran’s Space Dreams Derailed by Strikes
long_video.narration: Satellite imagery confirms heavy damage to Iran’s Space Research Centre after recent strikes. This blow could stall their tech aspirations for years. Geopolitics meets the final frontier—what’s next?

long_video.headline: Samsung vs. Chrome: The Browser Battle Heats Up
long_video.narration: Samsung Browser’s upcoming AI and multitasking features aim to challenge Chrome’s dominance. With 3.8 billion users, can Samsung carve out its space? The browser wars are back!

long_video.headline: Fed’s Dilemma: Rates, War, and Economic Uncertainty
long_video.narration: As the Fed holds rates steady, the Iran conflict sends shockwaves through global markets. Oil prices, inflation, and growth hang in the balance. Can policymakers steer through the chaos?

long_video.headline: GreenBoost: Supercharging GPUs for AI’s Future
long_video.narration: This open-source Linux module lets NVIDIA GPUs tap into system RAM and NVMe storage, unlocking larger AI models. A game-changer for developers—here’s how it works.

long_video.headline: Kernel Anti-Cheats: Gaming’s Double-Edged Sword
long_video.narration: These ultra-powerful systems protect games from cheaters but operate at kernel level—raising privacy and security concerns. Are they worth the risk? We break it down.

long_video.headline: Employee Rights vs. Corporate Realities: A Court’s Verdict
long_video.narration: The Delhi High Court’s ruling against a brain tumor survivor sparks debate over worker protections. A harsh reminder of the clash between compassion and business.

long_video.headline: AI, Chips, and Wall Street’s Next Big Bet
long_video.narration: With AI chip sales forecast to hit $100+ billion, Broadcom and others are riding the wave. How will this tech gold rush reshape the market? The numbers don’t lie.
